content.problemStatement:
Given an 2D matrix mÃ—nm \times nmÃ—n  , return all its elements in **spiral order**.

Function DeclarationFunction NamespiralOrderspiralOrderspiralOrder â€“ This function returns the elements of a matrix in spiral order.

Parameters
matrixmatrixmatrix : A 2D array of integers with mmm rows and nnn columns.
Return Value
Returns an array containing all matrix elements in **spiral order**.
Constraints
1â‰¤m,nâ‰¤101 \leq m, n \leq 101â‰¤m,nâ‰¤10
âˆ’100â‰¤matrix[i][j]â‰¤100âˆ’100 \leq matrix[i][j] \leq 100âˆ’100â‰¤matrix[i][j]â‰¤100
The matrix is non-empty

Input Format

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers mmm and nnn â€” the number of rows and columns of the matrix.
The next mmm lines each contain nnn space-separated integers, representing the rows of the matrix.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line all elements of the matrix in **spiral order**, separated by spaces.

solution.approach:
We maintain **four boundaries**:


`top` â†’ starting row index
`bottom` â†’ ending row index
`left` â†’ starting column index
`right` â†’ ending column index
At each step:


Traverse from **left to right** along `top`. Then increment `top`.
Traverse from **top to bottom** along `right`. Then decrement `right`.
If there are rows remaining, traverse from **right to left** along `bottom`. Then decrement `bottom`.
If there are columns remaining, traverse from **bottom to top** along `left`. Then increment `left`.
Repeat this process until `top > bottom` or `left > right`.
